food_subgroup: Paneer cubes in spiced spinach-tomato gravy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Palak Paneer. Nearest parent is a dairy-vegetable curry. A new concept for Indian paneer-based dishes is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dairy (paneer) in vegetable curry sauce, North Indian style (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of palak paneer is 1 bowl (approximately 200g) — vivid dark-green spinach curry with soft white cubes of paneer, creamy and mildly spiced, served as a main dish with roti or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Palak Paneer — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, Entry F01040001
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in ICMR-NIN under entry F01040001 — Palak Paneer, standard North Indian preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate curry; paneer and spinach combination gives very low GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 624mg per bowl (200g) — elevated; restaurant palak paneer tends to be high in salt and cream; home cooking allows control.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparations and minimal cream. The high calcium (185mg/100g) and potassium content from spinach and paneer are beneficial for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~22) and very low carbohydrate content (7g/100g); glycaemic load per bowl is approximately 3 — negligible. High protein and fat slow any glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Excellent choice for diabetic patients — palak paneer has one of the lowest glycaemic loads of all Indian restaurant dishes. The high protein (15g per bowl) supports satiety and blood sugar control.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Spinach provides exceptional folate (88mcg DFE/100g) and vitamin A (320mcg RAE/100g); turmeric provides curcumin; paneer provides calcium for bone health.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Highly recommended for patients with rheumatoid arthritis — the combination of spinach's anti-inflammatory micronutrients, turmeric's curcumin, and paneer's calcium makes this one of the most beneficial curries for bone and joint health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Palak Paneer is one of India's most beloved vegetarian curries — soft, fresh paneer (Indian cottage cheese) cubes nestled in a thick, velvety, bright-green sauce made from blanched and puréed fresh spinach, cooked with onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, garam masala, and finished with a splash of cream or yogurt. The spinach sauce is smooth and intensely green, with the paneer providing a mild, creamy contrast. Nutritionally exceptional — very low GI, high calcium, high vitamin A, high folate, and rich in plant iron. Available at virtually all North Indian restaurants in Singapore.
common_user_queries: palak paneer vs dal makhani nutrition

confidence_reason: Exact match found in ICMR-NIN under entry F01040001 — Palak Paneer, standard preparation.
notes: Contains dairy (paneer and cream/yogurt) — not vegan or dairy-free. Cholesterol approximately 64mg per bowl (200g). Vitamin B12 present from dairy. High saturated fat from paneer and cream — patients with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size. Spinach oxalate reduces calcium bioavailability from spinach itself, but paneer calcium is well-absorbed.
